zh.lifeSummary: 當Jeanne Aliénor de Léon於大約1150年出生在法國布列塔尼時，她的父親Vicomte Hervé II de LÉON42歲，母親Sybille de BLOIS32歲。她和Eudes de PORHOËT III至少有1個女兒。她去世於1204年在法國布列塔尼莫比韓吉利爾斯享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jeanne Aliénor de Léon于大约1150年出生在法国布列塔尼时，她的父亲Vicomte Hervé II de LÉON42岁，母亲Sybille de BLOIS32岁。她和Eudes de PORHOËT III至少有1个女儿。她去世于1204年在法国布列塔尼莫比韓吉利爾斯享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Léon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Jeanne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine form of   Jean .

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jeanne Aliénor de Léon was born about 1150, in Brittany, France, her father, Vicomte Hervé II de LÉON, was 42 and her mother, Sybille de BLOIS, was 32. She had at least 1 daughter with Eudes de PORHOËT III. She died in 1204, in Guilliers, Morbihan, Brittany, France, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jeanne Aliénor de Léon est née environ 1150, à Bretagne, France, son père, Vicomte Hervé II de LÉON, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Sybille de BLOIS, avait 32 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Eudes de PORHOËT III. Elle est décédée en 1204, à Guilliers, Morbihan, Bretagne, France, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jeanne Aliénor de Léon um 1150 in Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Vicomte Hervé II de LÉON 42 und ihre Mutter Sybille de BLOIS 32. Sie hatte mit Eudes de PORHOËT III mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1204 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Guilliers, Morbihan, Bretagne, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne Aliénor de Léon è nata il circa 1150 nel Bretagne, Francia, sua padre, Vicomte Hervé II de LÉON, aveva 42 anni e sua madre, Sybille de BLOIS, aveva 32 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Eudes de PORHOËT III. Morì nel 1204, nel Guilliers, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia, all'età di 55 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jeanne Aliénor de Léonが約1150年にフランスブレタグンで生まれた時，父Vicomte Hervé II de LÉONは42歳，母Sybille de BLOISは32歳であった。彼女はEudes de PORHOËT IIIとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1204年にフランスブレタグンモービハングイリールズで55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jeanne Aliénor de Léon(이)가 대략 1150년 프랑스 브레타그니에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Vicomte Hervé II de LÉON(은)는 42세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sybille de BLOIS(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 Eudes de PORHOËT III(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1204년 프랑스 브레타그니 모르비핸 길리어스에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jeanne Aliénor de Léon nasceu aproximadamente 1150, em Bretagne, França, seu pai, Vicomte Hervé II de LÉON, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Sybille de BLOIS, tinha 32 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Eudes de PORHOËT III. Ela faleceu em 1204, em Guilliers, Morbihan, Bretagne, França, com 55 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jeanne Aliénor de Léon родилась около 1150 г. в местности бретагн, Франция, её отцу по имени Vicomte Hervé II de LÉON было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Sybille de BLOIS, 32. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Eudes de PORHOËT III. Она умерла в 1204 г. в местности гуиллиирс, морбихань, бретагн, Франция в возрасте 55 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jeanne Aliénor de Léon nació aproximadamente 1150, en Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Vicomte Hervé II de LÉON, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Sybille de BLOIS, tenía 32 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Eudes de PORHOËT III. Murió en 1204, en Guilliers, Morbihan, Bretagne, Francia, a la edad de 55 años.
